title: UtensilMenace
description: A digital forensics scenario involving the analysis of a potentially malicious executable and its impact on the system.

title: WorkFromHome
description: Analyze forensic artifacts to trace an attacker progression from initial social engineering and remote access to a Sticky Keys privilege escalation.

title: ResourcePacks
description: Reconstruct an attack timeline by analyzing forensic artifacts to identify a UAC bypass, WMI persistence, and backdoor user creation techniques.

title: YARA Trap
description: Investigate attacker behavior by analyzing Windows artifacts to identify persistence, privilege escalation, and lateral movement using MFTECmd, PECmd, BitsParser, and registry analysis tools.

title: Spooler - APT28
description: Hunt browser downloads, MFT records, and Prefetch to unmask the initial dropper and rebuild the attack timeline.

title: Perfect Survey
description: Investigate the compromised web server, which also functions as the domain controller for the Wowza Sport environment. You are expected to analyze logs, identify the source and method of the attack, and assess the potential impact on both the website and the organization's Active Directory infrastructure

title: LFI Escalation
description: Investigate the full scope of the compromise—tracing how the attacker gained access from the website, what actions they performed, and how they established persistence on the system.

title: KioskExpo7
description: Investigate how a physical kiosk breakout led to full system compromise using host and forensic artifacts.

title: RoastToRoot
description: Analyze network traffic to reconstruct a complete domain compromise attack chain, from AS-REP Roasting and Kerberoasting through privilege escalation, lateral movement, and data exfiltration using rclone.

title: RaaS Unfold - RansomHub
description: A ransomware empire built on the ashes of its predecessors, trace its origins, expose its operators, and unfold its playbook.

title: WorkFromHome
description: Wowza Enterprise (Wowza Inc.) has hired you to investigate an incident involving a workstation used by Otello John, which is believed to have been compromised by a threat actor. The attacker reportedly changed the desktop wallpaper to a questionable image. The IT team has already provided you with initial information, and it is your duty to report back your findings.

title: SHAW
description: Investigating ClickFix incident by correlating Browser history, PowerShell Script Block Logging, Prefetch and PowerShell log together

title: Master Investigator
description: Investigating incident starting from phishing, privilege escalation to domain compromise with Active Directory Certificate Services.

title: Awesome Splunk and Elastic SIEM Practice Labs
description: A list of labs with Splunk and Elastic from various CyberSecurity Training platforms such as CyberDefenders, BlueTeamLabs Online (BTLO), TryHackMe, DFIR Labs, Xintra, Ace Responder, and more

title: Awesome Cloud Investigation Labs
description: A list of cloud investigations from various cybersecurity platforms such as Blue Team Labs Online (BTLO), CyberDefenders, HackTheBox Sherlocks (HTB), CyberWarFare Labs Infinity (CWL Infinity) and so on

title: Pulse Log Generator
description: A blue team training tool that generates realistic, scenario-based server logs for SOC analysts, DFIR students, and CTF challenge authors. Produce authentic attack logs across six log formats with configurable difficulty levels and built-in answer keys.

title: Design your first Windows Forensics Lab and make it decent!
description: A blog about making your very own Windows Forensics lab, not deep dive too much into technical aspect but flavors of Windows Forensics scenarios

title: Using Git Commits as a Persistence Mechanism
description: Research about how red teamer and threat actor can use git history to hide their payload and execute it with the help of other persistence mechanism

title: RMM Forensics Cheat Sheet
description: A repository contains forensic artifacts and indicators for Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools commonly observed in threat actor operations. These cheat sheets are compiled from hands-on research in controlled lab environments to support defensive security operations.

title: Deep dive into AnyDesk Investigation & Forensics on Windows
description: Research about AnyDesk RMM Investigation & Forensics on Windows from binary to connection, file transfer and unattended access.

title: Deep dive into RustDesk RMM Investigation & Forensics on Windows
description: Research about RustDesk RMM Investigation & Forensics on Windows from binary to connection, file transfer and unattended access.

title: RustDesk Decryptor
description: A forensic tool for decrypting RustDesk configuration files. Designed for security research, penetration testing, and digital forensics investigations.
